source_domains.label: Default object cache runtime and public cache API
Object-cache drop-in compatibility shims and bootstrap

cases.focus: stored false values are distinguishable from misses through the found reference
set, replace existing/missing, delete existing/missing, and default group semantics
batch helper return maps, false values, and missing keys
numeric mutation, missing-key false, nonnumeric coercion, and zero clamping
object values are cloned on write and on read
feature reporting, group flush, runtime flush alias, full flush, close, and non-persistent group no-op
multisite blog prefixes isolate local groups while global groups remain shared
cache addition suspension blocks add() while set() still writes
reset clears non-global groups, keeps globals, emits deprecated hooks, and preserves magic property compatibility
external object-cache drop-in bootstrap, compat function installation, and global group registration
cache-compat batch helpers delegate to a legacy drop-in's single-key functions
legacy drop-ins report unsupported features and doing-it-wrong flush fallbacks do not mutate storage
salted compat helpers store salt/data envelopes and reject stale or malformed cache entries
compat switch delegates to a drop-in method when present and falls back when absent

native_boundaries.reason: The runtime probe exercises WordPress's default request-local WP_Object_Cache implementation. Real persistent backends remain behind the object-cache.php drop-in boundary.
The probe defines MULTISITE and a deterministic blog ID so local group prefixes, global groups, and switch_to_blog behavior are observable without a full multisite bootstrap.

The drop-in probe supplies a deliberately small object-cache.php that implements legacy single-key APIs while omitting newer batch, feature, salted, flush, and switch functions so cache-compat.php installs its shims.
Unsupported flush compat paths and reset deprecations are captured through deterministic PHP hook/test-double loggers rather than emitting runtime notices.
wp_cache_get() found-reference behavior is retained as native PHP reference output; a Haxe port must model this at the WordPress-facing PHP ABI boundary.
WP_Object_Cache clones object values on write and read. This fixture records the PHP-visible behavior; deeper Haxe object identity lowering remains a PHP target/runtime concern.

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side is a copied WordPress oracle source tree until selected pure option/cache helpers move to Haxe parity candidates.
The drop-in probe uses a deterministic legacy object-cache.php test double. Redis/Memcached-like backends, eviction, TTL persistence, and networked cache failures remain later compatibility matrices.
Invalid cache-key _doing_it_wrong branches trigger early translation loading and are deferred to avoid mixing l10n bootstrap coverage into this storage/cache fixture.
This fixture covers seed traces. Full upstream cache PHPUnit parity remains a domain-level closure requirement.
